steps.title: Spot the contradiction that never changes
steps.content: Harris's anecdotes shift: the other unaffected passenger moves from the captain to the second mate to one other man to possibly no one. Yet Harris himself is always unwavering and healthy. This instability in who else was well, combined with his stability in being well, signals he's prioritizing his own boast over accuracy.

steps.title: Recognize the deliberate embellishment
steps.content: Harris changes the details of who was sick, but maintains his brag about his own wellness—exactly what choice C states. A invents future worry, B ignores his active embellishing, and D focuses on companionship rather than wellness. That matches choice C — C is correct.
